purpose: Report-only guardrail policy for determining whether a future WellFit pull request would be eligible for automatic merge consideration. In the current small policy tier, docs/register/validator build, commit, PR handoff, and narrowly scoped safe repair may be allowed elsewhere, but this policy never authorizes or performs a merge by itself.

pr_creation_allowed.meaningWhenActivated: An agent may create a reviewable pull request for allowed docs/register/validator/governance work only.
pr_creation_allowed.activationRequirement: A later explicit policy activation may make this the first meaningful next state; defining it here does not grant the current runner PR-creation authority.

requiredEvidence: all required local checks passed with exact command/result evidence
machine-readable GitHub required checks are present and successful
branch protection and review-rule evidence are satisfied
PR is mergeable against the base branch
PR diff review reports no runtime/protected path findings
merge_recommendation_allowed.meaningWhenActivated: An agent may recommend merge only when full machine-readable local and GitHub evidence is green.
merge_recommendation_allowed.activationRequirement: May activate only after pr_creation_allowed is active and full machine-readable local/GitHub evidence, branch protection, review rules, and mergeability evidence are proven.

auto_merge_allowed.meaningWhenActivated: Real auto-merge remains disabled until a separate explicit human approval enables it after machine-readable GitHub checks, branch protection, review rules, mergeability evidence, and human approval are proven.

separationRules: PR creation is not merge permission.
Missing, pending, skipped, unknown, failed, or environment-blocked checks block merge recommendation and auto-merge.

Runtime, protected, product, Unity, deployment, package, Firebase, GitHub workflow, token/NFT/wallet/payment/betting/reward-authority/mission-authority/health/child/location/camera/biometric/consent/privacy/legal/compliance changes remain blocked.

humanApprovalAlwaysRequiredFor: any package.json or package-lock.json change without explicit approval
any app, component, lib, function, Firestore rules, or Unity/WellFitBuddyAR change
token, NFT, wallet, payment, betting, reward authority, mission completion authority, health, child, location, privacy, legal, compliance, Unity, or PR #13 topics
failed, missing, skipped, or unknown required checks
more changed files, additions, or deletions than the configured maximum

stages.permission: Analyse, dry-runs, reports, eligibility decisions, and governance validation only.
mustNot: create pull requests from a runner
stages.currentStatus: Baseline safe mode; active for auto-merge itself.

stages.permission: Exactly one already-human-approved docs/register/check-script task may use a task branch, commit, and PR handoff within allowlisted governance paths.
requiredEvidence: json validation for changed policy/register files
stages.currentStatus: Allowed only for the narrow approved docs/register/check-script runner scope.

stages.permission: Only safe docs/register JSON parse/format repair may be attempted inside approved docs/register scope, with same-branch commit and rerun evidence.
mayChange: docs/register JSON-format evidence files already within approved task scope
mustNot: rewrite scripts outside the approved validator scope
requiredEvidence: rerun failed check and full required checks
stages.currentStatus: Allowed only as narrow evidence repair where the safe-repair policy explicitly permits it.

stages.permission: Produce merge/deploy eligibility reports that show present and missing evidence.
requiredEvidence: GitHub required-check status summary when available
mergeability and review evidence summary when available
deploy evidence gap summary when deploy is discussed
stages.currentStatus: Report-only visibility target; no actual merge or deploy.

stages.permission: Optional future assistant may prepare human-facing merge checklist, command suggestion, or handoff after explicit owner approval.
mustNot: merge with missing checks or reviews
merge protected/runtime scope without named review evidence
stages.currentStatus: Not active; future optional stage only.

stages.permission: Optional future assistant may prepare deploy checklist or handoff only after explicit owner approval, hosted CI evidence, rollback plan, environment separation, and protected-scope exclusion.
mustNot: change Firebase/package/workflow/deploy config unless separately approved
stages.currentStatus: Not active; future optional stage only.

currentRelease.scope: Docs/register/check-script PR handoff and safe docs/register JSON-format repair may be allowed by adjacent policies; merge/deploy eligibility may be reported only. No actual merge or deploy is authorized.

futureOptionalStages.2.0: Manual merge assistant after separate explicit owner approval only.
futureOptionalStages.3.0: Deploy assistant after separate explicit owner approval, hosted CI evidence, rollback plan, environment separation, and protected-scope exclusion only.

activationDecision.reason: Keep auto-merge disabled until GitHub checks, review evidence, and mergeability are reliably readable and enforceable.
activationDecision.effectiveScope: AUTO_MERGE_ELIGIBLE and any merge/deploy eligibility output remain report-only signals; no merge, approval, auto-merge enablement, deploy, repository-setting change, environment mutation, or PR close is authorized.
activationDecision.autoRepairPolicyDependency: Safe docs/register/JSON-format repair may be allowed by project-register/auto-repair-policy.json, but the auto-merge policy treats it only as evidence and still does not authorize merge.
activationDecision.discrepancyStatement: Desired future direction may include manual merge and deploy assistants, but current safety evidence supports only Stage 1.3 report-only eligibility for those actions.

governanceProtectedTopicWordingPolicy.meaning: AUTO_MERGE_ELIGIBLE=false is an acceptable safe report-only result for governance/documentation diffs that mention protected-topic words only as boundaries.
